Print or install a shell completion script

Usage:
  laurel completions [--json] [--shell <auto|bash|zsh|fish|pwsh>] [shell-or-action] [install-shell]

Arguments:
  shell-or-action     Target shell (bash, zsh, fish, pwsh) or `install`
  install-shell       Optional install target shell: auto, bash, zsh, fish, or pwsh

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `laurel new post -- --config`.

Options:
  -j, --json          No-op for `completions`; accepted so the global `--json` flag does not error here. The output is always shell-script text
  --shell <auto|bash|zsh|fish|pwsh>  Shell to install completions for: auto, bash, zsh, fish, or pwsh
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --json → LAUREL_COMPLETIONS_JSON

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel completion bash >> ~/.bashrc         # singular alias
  laurel completions bash >> ~/.bashrc
  laurel completions zsh > ~/.zsh/_laurel
  laurel completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/laurel.fish
  laurel completions install                 # install for the detected shell
  laurel completions install --shell zsh     # install under a user-writable zsh path
